content:
The Chase Bliss Audio Clean is a compressor pedal with all-analog signal path complemented by digital controls. Hybrid design provides flexibility and precision without compromising sound integrity. Targets both subtle compression and pronounced effects. Suited for studio and live performance environments.

Key Features: All-analog signal path with digital controls; versatile compression for various styles; intuitive UI; high-quality components; pedalboard-friendly.

content.q: Is the Chase Bliss Audio Clean compressor pedal suitable for both live performance and studio use?
content.a: Yes — designed for both, with stereo, analog, two-stage compression.

content.q: Does the Chase Bliss Audio Clean work well with both electric guitar and bass?
content.a: Yes — versatile for both, providing smooth compression and enhanced dynamic range.

content.q: What power supply does the Chase Bliss Audio Clean require?
Can the Chase Bliss Audio Clean be used in a stereo setup?
How does the Chase Bliss Audio Clean differ from other compressor pedals?
content.a: Innovative two-stage compression and analog circuitry; unique blend of warmth and clarity.

content.verification_note: Video review by Rhett Shull on his channel: 'How Did They Even Think Of This? Chase Bliss Clean'
content.verbatim_quote: Okay, so to start off with, Clean is without a doubt an amazing sounding compressor. It's really versatile. It's actually quite easy to use. I think it's pretty beginner-friendly... if you're someone that likes to use pedals for sound design or to mix with, the Clean is an amazing option because it's true stereo in and out. You can actually use it like an outboard rack compressor like my Distressors up there, for example. You can put drums through this, put vocals through this, put your entire mix through this, and use it to do basic compression, gluing things together, to hard slammed limiting, to adding Distortion to EQ — like it's a really powerful tool for mixing and for sound design.
content.summary: Rhett positions Clean as an amazing compressor for sound designers and mix-engineers, NOT for guitarists who only want subtle dynamics-evening compression — for that he says Clean is 'underutilized' and recommends UA 1176, JHS Pulp N Peel as cheaper alternatives. Highlights features: stereo in/out, MIDI in/out, expression+CV, side-chain input.

citation_targets.creator: Rhett Shull
citation_targets.video_title: How Did They Even Think Of This? Chase Bliss Clean

content.verification_note: Visible on her pedalboard from the London show
Demo video by Emily Hopkins of the Chase Bliss Clean compressor
content.summary: Harpist demos the pedal — notable because most compressor pedal demos are guitar-focused; harp use case validates Clean's versatility for non-traditional inputs.

to_gear.gear: Chase Bliss Audio / ZVEX Bliss Factory

cross_source_match_note: These three are also discussed by Rhett Shull (artist_usage) and by u/Twinningses in the Reddit review. Strong cross-source corroboration — high confidence Cali76 and Empress are the standard reference compressors to compare Clean against.
